page: Microsoft Azure F16als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine belonging to the Falsv6 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, though it is Premium Storage capable.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core stress-ng CPU performance ranks in the top tier, while the multi-core stress-ng performance falls within the average range. Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores are strong, placing in the top 25%. For specialized tasks, the processor's support for AVX-512 vector instructions and cryptography extensions contributes to strong bzip3 decompression and image processing speeds. Large-block memory bandwidth reads and writes operate at average speeds of 75.86 GB/sec and 55.97 GB/sec, respectively. In language model inference, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 350.51 tokens/second for small 135M models, though performance scales down to average levels for medium 7B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F16als_v6 offers a high-compute, low-memory profile. By limiting memory allocation to 2.0 GB per core and omitting local storage, the instance reduces overhead for workloads that are primarily CPU-bound rather than memory-intensive. This resource distribution makes it highly suitable for compute-intensive tasks such as software compilation, batch processing, image processing, web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference.
description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v6 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by a dedicated 16-core AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. Featuring an x86_64 architecture with 32.0 GB of RAM, this instance provides a lean 2.0 GB of memory per core and does not include local storage or GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core Geekbench scores, alongside efficient AVX-512 and cryptography instruction execution. Memory bandwidth and database operations perform at average levels. Due to its high compute-to-memory ratio, the F16als_v6 is a cost-effective choice for CPU-bound workloads such as software compilation, image processing, web serving, and small-scale language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v6 is a compute-optimized VM featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC cores at 3.7 GHz and 32 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier single-core performance for CPU-bound workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16als_v6 is a 16-vCPU compute-optimized VM with an AMD EPYC CPU and 32 GB RAM, built for CPU-intensive workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, compute-optimized virtual machine with dedicated AMD EPYC cores and a lean memory profile for CPU-bound workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V74 CPU cores operating at 3.7 GHz
32.0 GB of system memory providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong Geekbench scores
Supports AVX-512, vector, and cryptographic hardware acceleration
No bundled local storage or GPUs, optimized for external Premium Storage
Best suited for software compilation, web serving, and lightweight inference
